headline: Microsoft confirmation cycle. Q1 2026 tech-sector AI-attributed layoff narrative now mainstream in financial press; Microsoft contribution confirmed at ~9,000 (8,750 voluntary buyouts). Eightfold AI (used by Microsoft) sued under FCRA for opaque candidate ranking — adjacent hiring discrimination signal. Satya Nadella expected to testify in OpenAI trial as Microsoft co-defendant — verdict-pending evidence not yet scored. No new entity-specific events beyond what prior cycle scored. Sub-threshold confirmation.
key_evidence: Q1 2026 tech-sector layoffs: 78,557; 47.9% AI-attributed. Microsoft contribution ~9,000 confirmed (8,750 voluntary buyouts).
Eightfold AI (AI hiring vendor used by Microsoft) sued under FCRA for opaque candidate ranking — class action.
Satya Nadella expected to testify in Musk v. Altman trial as Microsoft co-defendant ($150B at stake).
$725B collective Big Tech capex vs. mass layoffs framing now mainstream financial-press narrative.
Pentagon classified AI deal participation continues (already scored).

evidence.finding: 20K combined Meta+Microsoft cuts framed as AI-labor-crisis catalyst; Microsoft buyout structure noted as voluntary.
Q1 2026 sector total: 78,557 tech layoffs with 47.9% AI-attributed.
Microsoft voluntary buyout structure analysis — first-of-its-kind for Microsoft, ~7% of US workforce.

scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.4 (-0.1). E1 Affective Resonance pressure from CEO comments to laid-off workers controversy continues; voluntary buyout structure is a partial offset.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.9 (-0.1). AC1 Responsiveness: voluntary structure preserves dignity better than forced layoff would have, but $725B capex vs. 9,000 cuts asymmetry creates AC2 Proportionality pressure.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.7 (-0.1). B3 Scope Clarity pressure from Pentagon classified deal participation; offset by voluntary-buyout transparency.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.7 (-0.1). Modest pressure on AB1 from CEO commentary controversy.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.9 (-0.1). I4 Values Alignment: Pentagon participation creates minor pressure; offset by buyout-structure transparency.

math_check.validator_check: Sub-threshold delta (-1.4); Established band sustained; no band crossing.

negativeSignals: 9,000 Microsoft layoffs (largest in company history); Eightfold AI FCRA suit affecting Microsoft hiring stack; Pentagon classified deal participation; CEO comments to laid-off workers continuing controversy.
positiveSignals: Voluntary buyout structure (vs. forced layoff); $145B AI infrastructure investment; remains highest-scoring Fortune 500 tech entity; transparency around buyout terms.
sector_context: Microsoft's voluntary buyout structure differentiates it favorably from Meta's involuntary 8,000-cut + 6,000-role-cancellation posture. Both are scored within the same sector-wide AI-attributed layoff narrative.
open_actions_resolved: Eightfold AI FCRA suit captured as adjacent risk signal — not yet scored as direct Microsoft evidence
open_actions_carried: Monitor Satya Nadella OpenAI trial testimony for governance disclosures
If Eightfold litigation produces Microsoft-specific findings, full reassessment
Buyout completion outcomes and post-buyout employee feedback: capture in subsequent cycles
